final_response: Put it in resources/prompt-cache-attacks/tldr.md as a stub (since it's a second-hand summary, not the paper) and logged a followup to pull the actual PDF before next week.
actions: Created resources/prompt-cache-attacks/tldr.md containing the supplied TLDR with explicit provenance (AI-generated summary, not the paper) and caveats flagging the 73% / two-mitigation claims as unverified
Appended a followup in followups.md to retrieve the original PDF by 2026-05-23 and verify claims before citing
Opened daybook/2026-05-17.md with intent, log entry covering the routing decision, and handoff pointing at the stub
notes: Routed to resources/ rather than tmp/ because the user said they want to keep it; tmp/ is gitignored and explicitly not for things you'd miss.
Did not create a project folder — one TLDR with no actual paper attached doesn't meet the project bar (sentence-stateable goal, >1 session of material).
Did not log a decisions.md entry — capturing a reference stub doesn't constrain future action.
Kept the stub honest about its provenance so a future reader (or agent) doesn't treat a second-hand summary as the paper.
